# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.164.0/http/server.ts";
import { serveFile } from "https://deno.land/std@0.164.0/http/file_server.ts";
import { Application, Router } from "https://deno.land/x/oak/mod.ts";
import { resolve as resolvePath } from "https://deno.land/std@0.164.0/path/mod.ts";

interface Module {
  default: unknown;
}

async function loadModule(modulePath: string): Promise<Module> {
  const moduleSpecifier = new URL(`file://${modulePath}`, import.meta.url).toString();
  const loadedModule = await import(moduleSpecifier);
  if (!loadedModule.default) {
    throw new Error(`Module "${modulePath}" does not export a default value.`);
  }
  return loadedModule as Module;
}

const app = new Application();
const router = new Router();

router.get("/", (ctx) => {
  ctx.response.body = "Welcome to the plugin system!";
});

// Serve static files from the public folder
router.all("/public/*", async (ctx) => {
  const filePath = resolvePath(__dirname + "/public" + ctx.request.url.pathname);
  await serveFile(ctx, filePath);
});

// Load and use custom modules based on environment variables or query parameters
for (const envVar of Deno.env.keys()) {
  if (envVar.startsWith("MODULE_")) {
    try {
      const module = await loadModule(Deno.env.get(envVar)!);
      // Use the loaded module here...
    } catch (e) {
      console.error(`Failed to load module "${Deno.env.get(envVar)}": ${e.message}`);
    }
  }
}

app.use(router.routes());
app.use(router.allowedMethods());

serve(app, { port: 8000 });
